decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.27°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat spreading away from the chip. I will increase the spreader conductivity by a factor of 1.5, which is within the allowed single-step ratio and bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat diffusion, reducing the thermal gradient and peak temperature at the chip location.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
High conductivity materials may be costlier or heavier in physical implementation, though not modeled here.
